title: Outside the Law

evidence.note: Published 1920; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

diy_links.label: Stanford Copyright Renewal DB — search “Outside the Law” (renewals ~1947, 1948, 1949)
Catalog of Copyright Entries (archive.org) — browse the Motion Pictures renewal-year volume by hand
US Copyright Office public records (1978+ renewals/assignments)

editorial: Outside the Law is a 1920 American crime film directed by Tod Browning for Universal Pictures, starring Priscilla Dean as a reformed jewel thief and Lon Chaney in a dual role, including the gangster 'Black Mike' Sylva. An early psychologically driven gangster picture, it marked Chaney's second pairing with Browning. As a 1920 US publication, the film print is public domain by expiry of the 95-year US copyright term; other layers remain undetermined.
